sections.title: Maturity Requirements
sections.text:
The tomatoes must be sufficiently developed and display satisfactory ripeness.
The development and state of maturity of the tomatoes must be such as to enable them to continue their ripening process and to reach the appropriate degree of ripeness.

sections.title: “Extra” Class
sections.text:
Tomatoes in this class must be of superior quality. They must have firm flesh and must be characteristic of the variety as regards shape, appearance and development.
They must be uniform in terms of size. Their colouring, according to their state of ripeness, must be such as to satisfy the requirements set out in Section 2.1.1 above.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING
sections.text: When sized by diameter, size is determined by the maximum diameter of the equatorial section. Sizing does not apply to trusses of tomatoes. Sizing is not compulsory for Class II. Tomatoes are sized with one of the following options:

sections.title: Class I
sections.text: Tomatoes in this class must be of good quality. They must have reasonably firm flesh and must be characteristic of the variety as regards shape, appearance and development. They must be uniform in terms of size. They must be free of cracks and visible greenback. The following slight defects, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package: a slight defect in shape and development; a slight defect in colouring; slight skin defects; very slight bruises. Furthermore, “ribbed” tomatoes may show: shallow healed cracks not more than 1 cm long; no excessive protuberances; small umbilicus but not suberization; suberization of the stigma up to 1 cm2; a linear scar no longer than two thirds of the greatest diameter of the fruit.
sections.retrieval_text:
2 > 2.2 > Class I
number=2.2.2
level=2
Tomatoes in this class must be of good quality. They must have reasonably firm flesh and must be characteristic of the variety as regards shape, appearance and development. They must be uniform in terms of size. They must be free of cracks and visible greenback. The following slight defects, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package: a slight defect in shape and development; a slight defect in colouring; slight skin defects; very slight bruises. Furthermore, “ribbed” tomatoes may show: shallow healed cracks not more than 1 cm long; no excessive protuberances; small umbilicus but not suberization; suberization of the stigma up to 1 cm2; a linear scar no longer than two thirds of the greatest diameter of the fruit.

page_spans.text: Tomatoes in this class must be of good quality. They must have reasonably firm flesh and must be characteristic of the variety as regards shape, appearance and development. They must be uniform in terms of size. They must be free of cracks and visible greenback. The following slight defects, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package: a slight defect in shape and development; a slight defect in colouring; slight skin defects; very slight bruises. Furthermore, “ribbed” tomatoes may show: shallow healed cracks not more than 1 cm long; no excessive protuberances; small umbilicus but not suberization; suberization of the stigma up to 1 cm2; a linear scar no longer than two thirds of the greatest diameter of the fruit.

sections.title: Class II
sections.text: This class includes tomatoes which do not qualify for inclusion in the higher classes, but satisfy the minimum requirements specified in Section 2.1 above. They must have reasonably firm flesh (but may be slightly less firm than in Class I) and must not show unhealed cracks. The following defects, however, may be allowed, provided the tomatoes retain their essential characteristics as regards the quality, the keeping quality and presentation: defects in shape, development and colouring; skin defects or bruises, provided the fruit is not seriously affected; shallow healed cracks not more than 3 cm in length for round, ribbed or oblong tomatoes. Furthermore, “ribbed” tomatoes may show: more pronounced protuberances than allowed under Class I, but without being misshapen; one umbilicus; suberization of the stigma up to 2 cm2; fine blossom scar in elongated form (like a seam).

Furthermore, “ribbed” tomatoes may show: more pronounced protuberances than allowed under Class I, but without being misshapen; one umbilicus; suberization of the stigma up to 2 cm2; fine blossom scar in elongated form (like a seam).

page_spans.text: This class includes tomatoes which do not qualify for inclusion in the higher classes, but satisfy the minimum requirements specified in Section 2.1 above. They must have reasonably firm flesh (but may be slightly less firm than in Class I) and must not show unhealed cracks. The following defects, however, may be allowed, provided the tomatoes retain their essential characteristics as regards the quality, the keeping quality and presentation: defects in shape, development and colouring; skin defects or bruises, provided the fruit is not seriously affected; shallow healed cracks not more than 3 cm in length for round, ribbed or oblong tomatoes. Furthermore, “ribbed” tomatoes may show: more pronounced protuberances than allowed under Class I, but without being misshapen; one umbilicus; suberization of the stigma up to 2 cm2; fine blossom scar in elongated form (like a seam).

sections.title: UNIFORMITY
sections.text: The contents of each package must be uniform and contain only tomatoes of the same origin, variety or commercial type, quality and size (if sized). The ripeness and colouring of tomatoes in “Extra” Class and Class I must be practically uniform. In addition, the length of “oblong” tomatoes must be sufficiently uniform. The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents.

The ripeness and colouring of tomatoes in “Extra” Class and Class I must be practically uniform. In addition, the length of “oblong” tomatoes must be sufficiently uniform. The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents.

sections.title: PACKAGING
sections.text: Tomatoes must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly. The materials used inside the package must be new, clean, and of a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage to the produce. The use of materials, particularly of paper or stamps bearing trade specifications is allowed, provided the printing or labelling has been done with non-toxic ink or glue. Tomatoes shall be packed in each container in compliance with the Recommended International Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruit and Vegetables (CAC/RCP 44-1995).

sections.title: PRESENTATION
sections.text: The tomatoes may be presented as follows: (i) as individual tomatoes, with or without calyx and short stalk; (ii) as trusses of tomatoes, in other words, in entire inflorescence or part of inflorescence, where each inflorescence or part of each inflorescence should comprise at least the following number of tomatoes: 3 (2 if prepackaged), or in the case of trusses of “cherry” tomatoes, 6 (4 if prepackaged).

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text: The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CODEX STAN 193-1995). The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text: It is recommended that the produce covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the Recommended International Code of Practice - General Principles of Food Hygiene (CAC/RCP 1 -1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CAC/RCP 53-2003), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice. The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria for Foods (CAC/GL 21-1997).

sentences.original: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of tomatoes grown from Lycopersicon esculentum Mill, of the Solanaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging.

sentences.original: The tomatoes may be presented as follows: (i) as individual tomatoes, with or without calyx and short stalk; (ii) as trusses of tomatoes, in other words, in entire inflorescence or part of inflorescence, where each inflorescence or part of each inflorescence should comprise at least the following number of tomatoes: 3 (2 if prepackaged), or in the case of trusses of “cherry” tomatoes, 6 (4 if prepackaged).

sentences.original: It is recommended that the produce covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the Recommended International Code of Practice - General Principles of Food Hygiene (CAC/RCP 1 -1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CAC/RCP 53-2003), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR TOMATOES (CODEX STAN 293-2008)
pages.summary: Página concluída com a identificação dos principais títulos hierárquicos numerados 1, 2, 2.1, 2.1.1, 2.1.2, 2.2 e 2.2.1. Texto associado fiel ao trecho da página. Seções 2.2 e 2.2.1 assinaladas como contínuas para a próxima página devido à interrupção visível no final da página.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 293
pages.summary: A página contém seções hierárquicas de nível 2 identificadas como Classes I e II sob a seção 2.2, e uma nova seção de nível 1, 3. PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING. As classes 2.2.2 e 2.2.3 têm conteúdo descritivo detalhado. Seções 2.2.2 e 2.2.3 continuam a partir da página anterior. A seção 3 inicia na página atual e continua para a próxima página.

A página 3 apresenta a seção 4 PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES com subseção 4.1 QUALITY TOLERANCES e dois subtítulos 4.1.1 “Extra” Class e 4.1.2 Class I, contendo os textos explicativos correspondentes. A hierarquia segue a numeração estruturada e os textos estão associados corretamente, sem continuidade explicitada com páginas adjacentes.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 293
pages.summary: A página 4 continua a seção 4 PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES com o subtítulo 4.1.3 Class II, indicando continuidade da seção iniciada anteriormente. Em seguida, apresenta a subseção 4.2 SIZE TOLERANCES e a nova seção 5 PROVISIONS CONCERNING PRESENTATION com suas subseções 5.1 UNIFORMITY, 5.2 PACKAGING (incluindo 5.2.1 Description of Containers) e 5.3 PRESENTATION. A página também inicia a seção 6 MARKING OR LABELLING com a subseção 6.1 CONSUMER PACKAGES e o subtítulo 6.1.1 Nature of Produce. Todos os títulos estão acompanhados do texto explicativo correspondente e a hierarquia está corretamente estruturada sem sinais de continuidade para página seguinte.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 293
pages.summary: Mapeamento da página 5 do documento CODEX STAN 293 identificou as seções 6.2 NON-RETAIL CONTAINERS com suas subseções 6.2.1 a 6.2.5, seguidas pelas seções principais 7 CONTAMINANTS e 8 HYGIENE. Foram capturados os títulos e textos associados com indicação de que não há continuidade entre páginas.
